headline: Israel says it has killed Iran's navy chief overseeing Strait of Hormuz blockade
summary: The strait, one of the world's busiest oil shipping channels, has been effectively blocked by Iran since the outbreak of the war last month.

headline: Yemen's Houthi movement stands ready to strike Red Sea again in solidarity with Tehran
summary: Houthi leader says group prepared to attack key waterway again, which would deepen global oil and economic crisis.

headline: Most Americans say US military action against Iran has gone too far, AP-NORC poll finds
summary: New polling shows public concern over extent of US involvement in Iran conflict.

headline: Olympic women's sport to be for biological females only from 2028
summary: International Olympic Committee announces women's category will be limited to biological females starting 2028.

headline: Sarah Mullally enthroned as Church of England's first female Archbishop of Canterbury
summary: Historic ceremony marks first woman to lead 85 million Anglicans worldwide as spiritual head.

headline: Russia in contact with US about new round of Ukraine peace talks
summary: Kremlin says discussions ongoing for peace settlement talks as soon as conditions allow.

headline: Pakistan continuing military operations against Afghanistan after Eid pause
summary: Foreign ministry confirms operations resumed following temporary pause for Islamic festival.

headline: Trump confirms May meeting with Putin
summary: US President schedules diplomatic meeting with Russian leader.

headline: European Parliament gives conditional approval to EU-US trade deal
summary: Trade agreement moves forward with parliamentary backing.

headline: Captured Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro to face New York court
summary: Maduro and wife Cilia Flores seized by US forces in Caracas, face drugs and weapons charges.
